title: Look & Touch: gaze-supported target acquisition
summary: This paper studies gaze-supported touch interaction on handheld devices and frames touch as a confirming gesture in target acquisition. It is relevant mainly as an interaction-technique reference and less as a central conceptual contribution to technologies of touch.

related.reason: Both are HCI interaction papers where touch is operationalized in experiments.

notes: A key idea here is that gaze suggests and touch confirms. At the moment this looks peripheral to the main conceptual direction, but it may still be useful as a comparison case.
